programmes.show_description: Deep Waters: Feature-length police drama. When a beautiful student is attacked on a campus, Frost has to decide whether it is an isolated incident or the start of a nightmare.
On the Make: Enduring '70s sitcom about a chirpy driver and his conductor pal. Stan takes Edna the clippie in when she is thrown out of her lodgings... and lives to regret it.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Aspects of Love: Classic British sitcom. When Simon arouses Oliver's suspicions and Oliver is the subject of a rumour in turn, both of these events lead to gossip in the village.
Kids' Outing: Enduring '70s sitcom. Blakey takes pleasure in making Stan and Jack take a school party on an outing, and he also accuses Stan of giving his nephew a girlie magazine.
Gray visits Perdy, who agrees to drop the custody petition in return for access to the baby. Ashley asks the Dolands about Mel's whereabouts, and Val and Pollard plan a wedding.
Gray tells Katie that he has painted the nursery. Melanie notices Ashley walks past Tenant's Cottage for the third time and tells Greg that they are under surveillance.
As Gail lies motionless at the bottom of the stairs, a terrified David runs out of the back door and makes his way to Tina's. Tony quizzes Carla about her relationship with Liam.
As Gail lies unconscious in intensive care, the doctors tell a panic-stricken David his mother is badly injured but should come round soon. Meanwhile, the police suspect foul play.
Penny for the Guy: When a ruthless and calculating kidnapper holds a supermarket chain to ransom with a young boy's life, Frost knows that he is facing his toughest deadline ever.
The Hospital Job: A search for stolen antiques becomes life-threatening for Mason and Wetherby, and prejudices are stirred by the arrival of a group of Taoist monks.
Ups and Downs: A cat burglar is on the prowl, targeting the wealthiest homes in the district. Carol catches the eye of a dashing visitor while Peggy plays matchmaker for David.
A Worm in the Bud: Feature-length drama. Barnaby finds himself in the midst of witchcraft, ancient potions, infidelity and land feuds when a woman's body is discovered in a wood.
Katie is livid when Gray continues to lie to her, so she pays Perdy a visit. Gabby visits Laurel and begs her step-mum to return home and Jo finds a buyer for her goats.
Pollard decides to surprise Val by bringing their wedding forward after he receives a large tax demand. Bob continues to be suspicious of Viv, and Jasmine gets a job at the pub.
The police quiz Jason about Gail's fall and reveal he is their only suspect, and Carla is caught off-guard when Tony makes an unexpected gesture during his dinner party.
Audrey quizzes Jason about his argument with Gail in a bid to find out what really happened. Carla laps up all the attention and Michelle frets about Ryan's trip with Nick.
Thursday's Children: Rachel, Joe and Carol go to Australia in search of Rosie and Carol's long-lost brother.
The Middle of Somewhere: Joe, Rachel and Carol find what they are looking for in Australia - but their search ends in tragedy - while Carol finds herself having to deliver a baby.
Cuckoo: DCI Vera Stanhope is called to the tragic scene of an unidentified teenage boy found dead in a sleepy coastal town.
Terminus: Crime drama. The winter of 1971 - the team are called to investigate the violent and peculiar murder of an Oxford college don when Thursday makes an unexpected discovery.
Penny for the Guy: When a ruthless and calculating kidnapper holds a supermarket chain to ransom with a young boy's life, Frost knows that he is facing his toughest deadline ever.
The 'L' Bus: Enduring '70s sitcom. When Stan needs to transport a new bed for Arthur and Olive to the house, he uses a passenger-free bus that normally employed for training drivers.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Neighbours: Classic British sitcom. Simon decides to make peace with Oliver, but his rival doesn't look ready to co-operate until a new resident causes a sleepless night for both.
The Canteen Girl: Enduring '70s British sitcom. Stan and Jack tease Inspector Blake when they are amused at the attention showered on new canteen girl Molly by their superior.
Katie is livid when Gray continues to lie to her, so she pays Perdy a visit. Gabby visits Laurel and begs her step-mum to return home and Jo finds a buyer for her goats.
Pollard decides to surprise Val by bringing their wedding forward after he receives a large tax demand. Bob continues to be suspicious of Viv, and Jasmine gets a job at the pub.
The police quiz Jason about Gail's fall and reveal he is their only suspect, and Carla is caught off-guard when Tony makes an unexpected gesture during his dinner party.
Audrey quizzes Jason about his argument with Gail in a bid to find out what really happened. Carla laps up all the attention and Michelle frets about Ryan's trip with Nick.
House Calls: Detective drama series. Two horrific murders for Frost to solve make as many demands on the man's compassion as on his professional expertise.
Thursday's Children: Rachel, Joe and Carol go to Australia in search of Rosie and Carol's long-lost brother.
The Middle of Somewhere: Joe, Rachel and Carol find what they are looking for in Australia - but their search ends in tragedy - while Carol finds herself having to deliver a baby.
Ring Out Your Dead: Feature-length drama. DCI Barnaby investigates the death of bellringers in the village of Midsomer Wellow and must solve a riddle before the killer strikes again.
Bob gets information about Viv from a witness about a specific date his wife was seen with Freddie. Jimmy confronts Lexi, and Eli damages a delivery for Matthew.
David fobs Audrey off when she confronts him about Gail's fall, then turns to a stunned Tina to back him up. Fiz returns from her holiday and Michelle says goodbye to Ryan.
The police quiz David about his past and take Tina in for questioning. Paul looks on enviously as Leanne and Dan make a date and Fiz visits Chesney at the foster home.
School of Hard Knocks: When an ex-Army schoolteacher takes his strict discipline too far, Joe must race to find a missing gun and a schoolboy bent on revenge against the tutor.
The Runaways: A gangster's accountant is on the run from his underworld bosses, and David finds an old jeep with a surprising history.
Dead If You Don't: Crime drama. A Brighton football stadium receives a terrifying demand that locks Grace into a frantic race to save countless lives and prevent a chilling death.
Prelude: Crime drama series. Endeavour's return to duty at Castle Gate with a newfound sobriety coincides with a gruesome discovery in a college garden linked to an Oxford orchestra.
